def: To get through a hard time to the end.
Dry and withered; also very thin.
Able to act or exist on its own; self-reliant.
To bring something new into use; also to lead in or insert.
A sports competition to find the best team or player in Europe.
Across the whole of Europe.
A device inside a computer that stores data permanently.
The art of taking pictures with a camera; also a photograph.
The punctuation mark (?) placed at the end of a question.
The solid base on which a building or idea rests.
Something given in return for a service or favour.
A substance or measure that counters a poison or problem.
The sense by which sound is perceived.
Relating to the mind or intellect.
To produce or bring something into existence.
To smile broadly, often in a knowing or cheeky way.
To willingly allow someone a pleasure or benefit.
Gentle and generous in nature.
A sticky substance that flows from certain trees.
The country where a person was born or belongs.
To move about a place without a fixed route.
A school where pupils live during term time.
Expressing meaning by saying the opposite, often humorously.
Not connected with or important to the matter.
A small case, such as one holding medicine; also a spacecraft cabin.
To strike the hands together in applause; also to gossip.
To join two or more things into one.
A state of ease and convenience, with modern amenities.
A particular arrangement of factors; also a group of stars.
A junction where traffic moves in a circle around a central island.
Presently happening; continuous.
Able to be done.
Having no power to change or influence something.
A means of communication; also a substance through which something acts.
A school for pupils between primary and upper secondary level.
A building or machine for grinding grain into flour.
A line where two pieces of fabric are sewn together.
The direction or region between north and west.
The legally allowed use of force to protect oneself.
A short prose story, longer than a short story but shorter than a novel.
A floor of a building above the ground floor.
Lasting or intended to last for a long time.
A person who robs ships at sea.
A person who gives religious sermons.
To deliberately annoy someone or trigger a reaction.
A violent attack in order to steal.
A formal decision agreed by an official body.
Feeling as if everything is spinning; giddy.
The marked area where a sport is played.
To make something steady and unlikely to change or fail.
A sudden forceful thrust; also a pile or stack.
To make great efforts to achieve something.
Going on for several days.
Having a natural ability to do something well.
A small body of still water.
Showing strong, forceful feeling.
To make something bigger.
To manage to bear something difficult.
To mistake one person or thing for another.
To decline firmly to give or do something.
To make someone angry or irritated.
An official rule that must be followed.
To blame someone for a fault or wrongdoing.
Watchful and alert to danger.
The act of being elected again to a post.
A single row of written or printed words.
A long, distinct period of history.
Serving no purpose; useless.
Higher or better than the usual level.
